pharmacology_explanation: Inhibits prostaglandin synthesis via irreversible acetylation of COX enzymes.
mechanistic_reasoning: Irreversibly inhibits COX-1 in platelets preventing TXA2 formation reducing aggregation.

pharmacology_explanation: Reversible inhibitor of COX-1 and COX-2 decreasing pro-inflammatory prostaglandins.
mechanistic_reasoning: Competitively binds the arachidonic acid binding site on the COX enzyme.

pharmacology_explanation: Longer acting NSAID acting via reversible COX inhibition.
mechanistic_reasoning: Sustained competitive inhibition of COX enzymes provides prolonged anti-inflammatory effects.

pharmacology_explanation: Potent inhibitor of inflammation and pain.
mechanistic_reasoning: Inhibits COX with some preference for COX-2 but also affects lipoxygenase pathway.

pharmacology_explanation: One of the most potent NSAIDs used for severe joint inflammation.
mechanistic_reasoning: Directly prevents formation of prostaglandins and suppresses polymorphonuclear leukocytes.
Suppresses leukotriene synthesis alongside standard prostaglandin inhibition.
Intense selective binding to COX-1 leads to rapid pain relief but high GI/bleeding risks.

pharmacology_explanation: Once-daily NSAID with reduced GI effects at standard doses.
mechanistic_reasoning: Preferential COX-2 inhibition preserves some stomach lining integrity at lower doses.
Extended half-life maintains steady-state inhibition of prostanoids but increases long-term toxicity.
Selective active site fitting blocks inflammatory COX-2 while sparing constitutive COX-1.
Intense reduction in inflammatory prostaglandins with significant safety warnings for heart failure.
Converted to active sulfide form in liver reducing direct local stomach irritation.
High potency inhibition of COX in localized ocular or joint tissues.
Lack of acidic moiety protects gastric mucosa prior to hepatic activation into 6-MNA.
Strongly lipophilic properties allow sustained synovial fluid concentration.
Reversibly inhibits COX unlike aspirin leading to longer action but less anti-platelet effect.
Rapid absorption and clearance necessitates frequent dosing to maintain anti-inflammatory effect.
Rapid onset of action due to balanced tissue penetration and potent COX-1/2 binding.
Maintains baseline suppression of prostaglandins allowing once daily or alternative day dosing.
Stimulates cartilage matrix synthesis via glycosaminoglycan support while inhibiting inflammatory COX.
